sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution approving the expenditure of an amount not to exceed $500 per
Councilmember, including $500 from Councilmember Lunaparra’s discretionary fund
and funds from any other Councilmembers who would like to contribute to the California
Triathlon Club (Cal Tri) for the Fall 2025 Bearathlon Triathlon event with funds
relinquished to the City’s general fund for this purpose.
sections.background:
The Bearathlon Triathlon, hosted by Cal Tri, is an annual endurance event that brings
together student-athletes, community members, and elite competitors from across the
region. As one of the premier collegiate triathlons on the West Coast, Bearathlon has
provided a competitive and accessible race experience for athletes of all backgrounds
while promoting fitness, community engagement, and environmental sustainability.
Beyond the exciting race atmosphere, the Bearathlon Triathlon unites a broad spectrum
of the community—local families, amateur athletes, and professional competitors—in a
single venue that showcases the best of Berkeley’s waterfront. This event not only
promotes a healthy, active lifestyle but also fosters neighborhood pride and supports
local businesses.
The event takes place at the Berkeley Marina, featuring a swim in the San Francisco
Bay, a bike course along the waterfront, and a scenic run through César Chávez Park.
Bearathlon has a longstanding history of fostering a welcoming environment for
participants of all skill levels, from first-time triathletes to national champions.
Additionally, the race serves as a critical fundraising source for the UC Berkeley
Triathlon Team, which offers training, mentorship, and leadership development
opportunities for student-athletes. With the cost of hosting the event rising since the
beginning of the COVID-19 pandemic, the team has struggled to barely break even
each year.
Cal Tri is one of the largest sports clubs at UC Berkeley, among the top 3 largest
triathlon teams in the nation, and one of the most decorated collegiate triathlon teams in
history. Since 1989, the team has grown to over 200 members, including National
Champions and Olympic hopefuls as well as first ever swimmers, cyclists, and runners

trying out the sport. The team has won two overall national titles, six women’s national

sections.current_situation:
Despite its success, the rising costs of hosting a large-scale endurance event—
including permitting fees, safety measures, and infrastructure—have placed a financial
strain on the organizing team. Specifically, ever-increasing city fees have caused the
Bearathlon organizers to consider moving the event to a location outside of Berkeley.
They are seeking financial support to offset the growing expenses associated with event
logistics, including but not limited to:
● Permit and safety fees (including police, waterfront usage, and road closures)
● Equipment rental for race setup and athlete support
● Hydration and nutrition stations for participants
● Medals, T-shirts, and awards to enhance athlete experience
With the cost of hosting the event exceeding $30,000, financial support to offset rising
city fees would help ensure Bearathlon’s continued success and allow the team to focus
on delivering a high-quality event without excessive financial burden or having to move
the event away from Berkeley.
FINANCIAL IMPLICATIONS
No General Fund impact. $500 is available from the Councilmember’s office budget
discretionary account.
sections.environmental:
Bearathlon prioritizes environmental responsibility by implementing waste reduction
strategies, such as using compostable race materials, minimizing single-use plastics,
and organizing post-race cleanup efforts to preserve the natural beauty of the Berkeley
Marina.

1. Resolution
CONTACT PERSON
Councilmember Cecilia Lunaparra, 510-981-7170
Jonah Gottlieb, Chief of Staff
2180 Milvia Street, Berkeley, CA 94704 ● (510) 981-7170 ● clunaparra@berkeleyca.gov
Page 3 of 3
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
SUPPORT FOR THE CALIFORNIA TRIATHLON CLUB’S FALL 2025 BEARATHLON
TRIATHLON
WHEREAS, the California Triathlon Club (Cal Tri), a registered 501c(3) organization is
one of the largest sports clubs at UC Berkeley, among the top 3 largest triathlon teams
in the nation, and one of the most decorated collegiate triathlon teams in history; and
WHEREAS, The Bearathlon Triathlon, hosted by Cal Tri, is an annual endurance event
that brings together student-athletes, community members, and elite competitors from
across the region; and
WHEREAS, rising costs, including increased city fees, could force Bearathlon
organizers to move the 2025 event away from Berkeley; and
WHEREAS, Councilmember Lunaparra has surplus funds in her office expenditure
account.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Berkeley City Council that up to $500
relinquished by Councilmembers – including $500 from Councilmember Lunaparra –
shall be granted to the California Triathlon Club for the 2025 Bearathlon Triathlon.
